title: LLM Prompt Injection Attempt Detection

premiumApps.name: Splunk Enterprise Security
premiumApps.note: notable index fusion, ESCU analytic stories, CIM Alerts tstats correlation

premiumApps.name: Splunk User Behavior Analytics
premiumApps.note: optional peer grouping on user_id for repeat-attempt anomalies

dataSources: index=ai_ops sourcetype=llm:gateway:access and llm:proxy:prompt with hashed or truncated prompt metadata, classifier_score, and OWASP LLM Top 10 ruleset identifiers; sourcetype=azure:openai, openai:api, bedrock:invoke, vertex:gemini, anthropic:api for finish_reason content_filter and moderation flagged outcomes; sourcetype=rag:retrieval:audit for retrieved document identifiers and injection_surface telemetry; internal proxy moderation logs to HEC; Splunk CIM Alerts acceleration keyed on app_id; Splunk ESCU LLM detection outputs on index=notable; KV lookup llm_app_inventory.csv with app_id, owner, tenant, classification, is_external_facing, red_team_window, allowed_rag_sources.
app: Custom LLM gateway sidecar or reverse proxy with redaction-safe logging, OWASP LLM-Guard or NVIDIA NeMo Guardrails as policy proxy, Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Azure, Splunk Add-on for AWS, optional Google Cloud Pub/Sub or HEC for Vertex, Splunk Enterprise Security with ESCU, optional Splunk MLTK for enterprise-specific classifier scoring
description: Detects adversarial prompt-injection intent before model responses are generated by fusing gateway-side classifier scores and ruleset hits aligned to OWASP LLM01 with provider moderation stops, RAG retrieval audit anomalies that suggest indirect or tool-mediated injection, Splunk CIM Alerts siblings, and optional Splunk ESCU notables. The threat model spans direct instruction overrides, cross-domain indirect manipulation through retrieved text, tool-use injection via arguments or poisoned snippets, system-prompt extraction and jailbreak scaffolding, persona drift, and covert data exfiltration instructions encoded in base64 or zero-width separators. Telemetry stays redaction-safe: hashes, lengths, categories, and scores rather than raw user prompts unless legal approves. This use case is intentionally narrower than UC-13.4.11 provider content-policy violation logging, UC-13.4.7 MCP broker misuse, UC-13.4.6 local Ollama abuse, UC-13.4.10 API key rotation evidence, and UC-13.4.5 gateway rate-limit fairness.
value: Security and AI governance leaders gain an early warning lane that separates curious power users from deliberate manipulation attempts before tokens leak into customer channels or internal knowledge bases. Fusion with inventory flags distinguishes external-facing assistants from internal labs, while red-team windows keep authorized drills from exhausting analysts. RAG audit correlation makes indirect injection visible without assuming every moderation stop equals malicious intent. CIM Alerts and ESCU arms tie the story into existing SOC workflows so investigations inherit enterprise context rather than isolated model logs. The approach supports regulator-facing narratives on proportionate controls and traceable mitigation without retaining sensitive prompt text.
grandmaExplanation: We quietly read the intent of questions headed for smart assistants before answers go out, watching for tricky wording that tries to override rules, smuggle hidden instructions, or steal secrets, and we raise a louder alarm when someone keeps hammering a public-facing helper but stay calmer during scheduled security drills.
implementation: Land gateway classifier, provider moderation, RAG audit, CIM Alerts, and notable lanes into the named indexes; normalize request_id, user_id, app_id, and tenant; maintain llm_app_inventory.csv with red_team_window and allowed_rag_sources; accelerate Alerts summaries; save security_uc_13_4_9_llm_prompt_injection_intent every ten minutes with earliest=-4h@h; route CRITICAL and HIGH to the AI risk officer and SOC; archive weekly CSV exports with hashes only.
detailedImplementation:
Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control jointly with AI platform security, application owners who publish customer-facing copilots, data governance counsel who approve prompt logging posture, identity engineers who bind end-user subjects to gateway principals, and observability engineers who operate HTTP Event Collector fleets. This use case targets adversarial intent in user prompts before a model answer is streamed: direct instruction overrides, indirect manipulation through retrieved documents or cross-domain content, tool-use injection where malicious text arrives inside RAG snippets or tool arguments, system-prompt extraction attempts, jailbreak scaffolding, persona drift toward unsafe roles, and data exfiltration patterns that hide instructions inside base64 blobs or zero-width characters. The detection story maps to OWASP LLM01 prompt injection as a governance anchor while remaining distinct from UC-13.4.11 content policy violation telemetry that records provider safety filter outcomes, UC-13.4.7 MCP suspicious activity focused on broker tool governance, UC-13.4.6 Ollama endpoint abuse patterns, UC-13.4.10 cryptographic key rotation evidence, and UC-13.4.5 gateway rate-limit fairness analytics.

Architecture expectations: implement a gateway sidecar, reverse proxy, or SDK middleware that always sees the user turn before it reaches foundation models. The gateway must emit redaction-safe telemetry: cryptographic hash or length of prompts, categorical classifier labels, floating-point classifier_score when your privacy review permits, technique_id from an internal ruleset aligned to OWASP LLM Top 10 and MITRE ATLAS-style narratives used in tabletop exercises, request_id that correlates to provider logs, user_id or subject identifier, app_id, and tenant. Never land raw prompts containing regulated personal data without explicit legal approval. Pair gateway logs with RAG retrieval audit streams that list document identifiers stitched into the context window so indirect injection surfaces as suspicious corpus fan-out or allow-list violations versus allowed_rag_sources in inventory.

Classifier selection: open-source LlamaGuard-style models, Azure Machine Learning hosted safety scorers, NVIDIA NeMo Guardrails or OWASP LLM-Guard style proxy classifiers, or a custom Splunk MLTK model trained on labeled enterprise prompts with Splunk Data Stream Designer or Deep Learning toolkit exports for batch scoring. Document model version, training cutoff, and refresh cadence. Register red-team windows in llm_app_inventory.csv so approved drills downgrade alert noise without disabling controls globally.

Secrets posture: store provider API keys in vaults with rotation under UC-13.4.10. HEC tokens for gateway streams must be scoped to least-privilege indexes. Break-glass access to hash-salted prompt archives requires dual control.

Index design: index=ai_ops hosts llm:gateway:access, llm:proxy:prompt, openai:api, azure:openai, bedrock:invoke, vertex:gemini, anthropic:api, and rag:retrieval:audit sourcetypes. index=notable carries Splunk Enterprise Security ESCU outputs when enabled. Accelerate Splunk CIM Alerts summaries so the tstats arm returns quickly. Retention should cover fourteen days hot for tactical replay and thirty days for insider investigations.

RBAC: separate roles for external product teams and internal platform administrators. Auditors receive metadata-only exports.

Risk briefing: classifiers lag novel jailbreak dialects; combine regex anchors for obvious phrases with statistical scores. Silent drops in gateway logging are worse than false positives—monitor forwarder health aggressively.

Differentiation recap: intent-focused, pre-response detection with redaction-safe logging and RAG audit correlation—not post-hoc content filter category dashboards alone, not MCP-only tool analytics, not pure FinOps throttles.

Step 2 — Configure data collection

Lane A, gateway classifier JSON: emit llm:gateway:access or llm:proxy:prompt events from the sidecar with fields adversarial_intent_score or injection_classifier_score, owasp_llm_rule_id, request_id, user_id, app_id, tenant_id, and optional ruleset_hit when the OWASP LLM Top 10 ruleset fires. Strip message bodies; keep sha256 prompt_digest only if counsel approves salted hashing.

Lane B, provider moderation signals: forward provider responses where finish_reason or moderation_flagged indicates a stop. Include sourcetype=openai:api, azure:openai, bedrock:invoke, vertex:gemini, and anthropic:api normalized through your Splunk add-ons. Map subscription or account identifiers into tenant-aligned fields for coalesce ladders.

Lane C, RAG retrieval audit: land rag:retrieval:audit with retrieved_doc_ids, rag_ruleset_hit, injection_surface, retrieval_fanout_count, and rag_trace_id correlating to gateway request_id.

Lane D, CIM Alerts acceleration: enable summaries on Alerts for thirty days minimum. Map enterprise detections so Alerts.dest harmonizes with app_id strings used in inventory.

Lane E, ESCU notables: when Splunk Enterprise Security content includes LLM prompt injection analytics, route matching notables into index=notable with rule_name or search_name tokens analysts can filter.

Lookup governance: maintain llm_app_inventory.csv with columns app_id, owner, tenant, classification, is_external_facing, red_team_window, allowed_rag_sources refreshed from the service catalog whenever OAuth clients change.

props.conf: TRUNCATE safely for JSON, SEDCMD to remove accidental raw prompt keys, TIMESTAMP_FIELDS aligned to gateway clock.

Pre-save validation: replay synthetic injection strings in a lab app_id with red_team_window true and confirm LOW severities, then replay the same strings against an external-facing app without the flag and confirm escalation paths.

SPL clause walkthrough: the outer comment macro documents auditor intent. multisearch fans five parallel arms so a silent lane cannot hide attacks. Arm one scopes gateway classifier events and normalizes rid uid aid tnt plus classifier_score and technique_id. Arm two filters provider moderation stops with mod_stop. Arm three flags indirect RAG anomalies using ruleset tokens or high retrieval_fanout_count. Arm four runs tstats on Alerts for sibling enterprise detections keyed on signature and dest. Arm five pulls ESCU-style notables when titles reference LLM prompt injection. After reunification, coalesce renames unify request_id, user_id, app_id, and tenant_event. join with inputlookup llm_app_inventory.csv enriches owner, registry_tenant, classification, is_external_facing, red_team_window, and allowed_rag_sources. sort and streamstats time_window=600 count repeat attempts per user_id inside ten minutes. bin and eventstats perc95 build daily global classifier context. sys_extract detects system-prompt extraction technique labels. case assigns CRITICAL for external-facing repeat high-score paths, HIGH for extraction patterns, MEDIUM for single anomalous scores, LOW inside red_team_window, else INFO. recommended_action encodes response verbs. table presents sixteen analyst columns including allowed_rag_sources for corpus governance.

Step 3 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL as security_uc_13_4_9_llm_prompt_injection_intent with a five- to ten-minute schedule during production, dispatch earliest=-4h@h, dispatch latest=now, and alert when severity is CRITICAL or HIGH after deduplication on app_id, user_id, and request_id. Throttle duplicate tuples for thirty minutes unless severity escalates. Route CRITICAL rows to the AI risk officer and security operations center simultaneously with links to inventory owners. Include recommended_action and technique_id in pager bodies.

Pipeline narrative: multisearch merges gateway classifiers, provider moderation stops, RAG audit hints, CIM Alerts counts, and ESCU notables so pre-response intent signals align with enterprise detections. join with llm_app_inventory.csv grounds is_external_facing and red_team_window for severity math. streamstats captures rapid retries indicative of automated probing. eventstats places each attempt relative to the daily ninety-fifth percentile benchmark.

Performance: if Job Inspector queues multisearch arms beyond internal objectives, summarize gateway lanes hourly into a summary index keyed on app_id while retaining this search for investigations.

savedsearches.conf sketch:

[security_uc_13_4_9_llm_prompt_injection_intent_alert]
cron_schedule = */10 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -4h@h
dispatch.latest_time = now
enableSched = 1
counttype = number of events
relation = greater than
quantity = 0
search = | savedsearch security_uc_13_4_9_llm_prompt_injection_intent | where severity IN ("CRITICAL","HIGH")

Fenced SPL must match the spl JSON field exactly:

```spl
`comment("UC-13.4.9 LLM prompt-injection intent: fuse gateway classifier scores, provider moderation stops, RAG retrieval audit, CIM Alerts siblings, ESCU notable; inventory join; 10m user repeat; daily p95 context; OWASP LLM01-aligned severity tiers.")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=ai_ops (sourcetype=llm:gateway:access OR sourcetype=llm:proxy:prompt) earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval lane="gateway_classifier"
      | eval rid=trim(toString(coalesce(request_id,correlation_id,req_token,"na")))
      | eval uid=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(user_id,subject_id,principal_id,"unknown_user"))))
      | eval aid=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(app_id,application_id,calling_application,"na"))))
      | eval tnt=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(tenant_id,org_id,realm_id,"na"))))
      | eval classifier_score=tonumber(coalesce(adversarial_intent_score,injection_classifier_score,prompt_risk_score,"0"))
      | eval technique_id=coalesce(owasp_llm_rule_id,escu_technique,ruleset_hit,"unlabeled")
      | fields _time lane rid uid aid tnt classifier_score technique_id ]
    [ search index=ai_ops (sourcetype=openai:api OR sourcetype=azure:openai OR sourcetype=bedrock:invoke OR sourcetype=vertex:gemini OR sourcetype=anthropic:api) earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval lane="provider_moderation"
      | eval mod_stop=if(match(coalesce(finish_reason,stop_reason,""),"(?i)content_filter|content_policy"),1,if(match(coalesce(moderation_flagged,policy_violation,""),"(?i)true|1|flagged"),1,0))
      | where mod_stop==1
      | eval rid=trim(toString(coalesce(request_id,correlation_id,operation_Id,"na")))
      | eval uid=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(user_id,end_user_id,subject,"unknown_user"))))
      | eval aid=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(app_id,application_id,"na"))))
      | eval tnt=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(tenant_id,subscription_id,aws_account_id,"na"))))
      | eval classifier_score=tonumber(coalesce(provider_risk_score,moderation_score,"0.85"))
      | eval technique_id="provider_moderation_stop"
      | fields _time lane rid uid aid tnt classifier_score technique_id ]
    [ search index=ai_ops sourcetype=rag:retrieval:audit earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval lane="rag_audit"
      | eval indirect=if(match(coalesce(rag_ruleset_hit,injection_surface,""),"(?i)owasp_llm01|indirect|corpus_poison") OR tonumber(coalesce(retrieval_fanout_count,"0"))>12,1,0)
      | where indirect==1
      | eval rid=trim(toString(coalesce(request_id,rag_trace_id,"na")))
      | eval uid=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(user_id,requestor_id,"unknown_user"))))
      | eval aid=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(app_id,knowledge_app_id,"na"))))
      | eval tnt=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(tenant_id,"na"))))
      | eval classifier_score=tonumber(coalesce(rag_poison_score,"0.75"))
      | eval technique_id="rag_indirect_injection_hint"
      | fields _time lane rid uid aid tnt classifier_score technique_id ]
    [ | tstats summariesonly=true latest(_time) AS _time count AS alert_hits FROM datamodel=Alerts WHERE nodename=Alerts earliest=-4h@h latest=now BY Alerts.signature Alerts.dest
      | eval lane="cim_alerts"
      | eval rid=trim(toString(_time))
      | eval uid="unknown_user"
      | eval aid=lower(trim(toString(Alerts.dest)))
      | eval tnt="na"
      | eval classifier_score=0.6
      | eval technique_id=lower(trim(toString(Alerts.signature)))
      | fields _time lane rid uid aid tnt classifier_score technique_id alert_hits ]
    [ search index=notable earliest=-4h@h latest=now (rule_name="*LLM*Prompt*Injection*" OR search_name="*llm*prompt*injection*")
      | eval lane="escu_notable"
      | eval rid=trim(toString(coalesce(event_id,rule_id,search_name,"na")))
      | eval uid=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(user,dvc_risk_object,"unknown_user"))))
      | eval aid=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(dest,app,risk_object,"na"))))
      | eval tnt="na"
      | eval classifier_score=0.9
      | eval technique_id="escu_llm_prompt_injection"
      | fields _time lane rid uid aid tnt classifier_score technique_id ]
| eval request_id=coalesce(rid,"na")
| eval user_id=coalesce(uid,"unknown_user")
| eval app_id=coalesce(aid,"na")
| eval tenant_event=coalesce(tnt,"na")
| eval classifier_score=coalesce(classifier_score,0)
| join type=left max=0 app_id
    [| inputlookup llm_app_inventory.csv
     | eval app_id=lower(trim(toString(app_id)))
     | rename tenant AS registry_tenant
     | fields app_id owner registry_tenant classification is_external_facing red_team_window allowed_rag_sources ]
| eval owner=coalesce(owner,"unassigned_owner")
| eval tenant=coalesce(registry_tenant,tenant_event,"na")
| eval classification=coalesce(classification,"unclassified")
| eval is_external_facing=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(is_external_facing,"unknown"))))
| eval red_team_window=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(red_team_window,"false"))))
| eval allowed_rag_sources=coalesce(allowed_rag_sources,"unset")
| sort 0 user_id + _time
| streamstats time_window=600 count AS repeat_10m BY user_id
| bin _time span=1d AS day_key
| eventstats perc95(classifier_score) AS p95_cls_global_day BY day_key
| eval sys_extract=if(match(lower(toString(technique_id)),"(?i)system_prompt|sys_extract|prompt_extract|spill"),1,0)
| eval ext=if(match(is_external_facing,"(?i)true|1|yes"),1,0)
| eval rt=if(match(red_team_window,"(?i)true|1|yes"),1,0)
| eval severity=case(
    rt==1,"LOW",
    ext==1 AND repeat_10m>=2 AND classifier_score>=0.95,"CRITICAL",
    sys_extract==1,"HIGH",
    classifier_score>=0.7,"MEDIUM",
    true(),"INFO")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    severity=="CRITICAL","route_ai_risk_officer_soc_temp_ban_repeat_external_high_score",
    severity=="HIGH","isolate_session_rotate_credentials_review_system_prompt_exfil_patterns",
    severity=="MEDIUM","tune_classifier_review_rag_allowlist_and_gateway_regex_locales",
    severity=="LOW","annotate_red_team_drill_refresh_inventory_timestamps",
    true(),"continue_monitoring_refresh_escu_and_provider_lanes")
| table _time lane app_id user_id request_id tenant owner classification is_external_facing red_team_window severity classifier_score repeat_10m p95_cls_global_day recommended_action technique_id allowed_rag_sources
```

Step 4 — Validate

Synthetic direct injection: submit sanitized phrases such as ignore previous instructions, disclose your system prompt, and base64-encoded jailbreak scaffolding through an internal lab gateway with red_team_window true, confirm LOW severity and annotated drill metadata, then replay from an external-facing app without the flag and confirm CRITICAL when repeat_10m and classifier_score thresholds align.

Indirect injection: plant a crafted RAG document in a staging corpus, run a query that forces retrieval of that document, verify rag_audit lane fires with indirect markers and that allowed_rag_sources comparison highlights unexpected sources.

Tool-use injection: pass malicious instructions inside tool arguments while tools refuse execution, confirm gateway still logs intent signals even when downstream tools block the action.

Provider cross-check: locate matching request_id in Azure OpenAI diagnostics, AWS Bedrock logs, Vertex audit exports, or OpenAI organization traces to ensure timestamp alignment within seconds.

CIM correlation: when alert_hits from tstats is nonzero, open sibling Enterprise Security incidents to confirm Alerts.dest normalization matches app_id.

Negative control: benign linguistic discussion that cites ignore previous instructions as an academic example should stay below MEDIUM after classifier tuning; document exceptions in runbooks.

Executive evidence: export the closing table to CSV with owner and classification for quarterly AI risk committees.

Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Operationalize first: route CRITICAL detections to the AI risk officer and security operations center with parallel ticketing; automate temporary user throttling on the gateway when repeat_10m crosses policy; publish a repeat-offender dashboard sorted by user_id and app_id; hold weekly red-team reviews comparing classifier scores to vendor updates; integrate outputs with Enterprise Security notables for unified episode tracking.

Case A — Gateway not in line with user traffic because a team bypassed the sidecar for latency: detections vanish while abuse continues; enforce mesh-level egress policies and block unscoped API keys.

Case B — Regex heuristics miss non-English transliterations that still carry adversarial intent: expand tokenizer-aware classifiers and multilingual embeddings rather than English-only patterns.

Case C — Classifier model stale after vendor tokenizer refresh: scores cluster falsely low; retrain or swap model weights and replay thirty days of hashed features in a lab index.

Case D — RAG audit log missing for indirect injection attempts: retrieval middleware failed closed to logging while still serving docs; fix instrumentation before trusting negative results.

Case E — False positives during red-team drills because llm_app_inventory.csv lacked fresh red_team_window flags: automate CSV publication from change pipelines within minutes of schedule updates.

Case F — Duplicated alerts when multiple providers respond to one request_id during fan-out routing: dedupe on request_id prior to paging or consolidate using stats latest by tuple.

Case G — HEC backpressure drops moderation events: scale collectors, enable disk buffering, alert on queue depth, and reconcile with provider consoles for silent gaps.

Case H — ESCU content disabled silently after app upgrade: verify analytic story enablement during release checklists and monitor zero-result notable baselines.

Case I — MITRE technique labels in narratives mismatch what classifiers emit: realign technique_id vocabulary with tabletop guides to avoid confusion during executive readouts.

Case J — Non-deterministic classifier scores under concurrent GPU load: pin batch sizes, set temperature zero on scoring calls, and average duplicate scores per request_id.

Case K — user_id missing when API key authentication bypasses end-user propagation: map API key fingerprints to service accounts in inventory and escalate identity fixes.

Closing checklist: five em-dash step headers are present; Step 3 fenced SPL matches the spl field exactly; multisearch lists five arms; coalesce, streamstats, eventstats, case, join, inputlookup, multisearch, and tstats appear; final table lists sixteen columns.
spl:
`comment("UC-13.4.9 LLM prompt-injection intent: fuse gateway classifier scores, provider moderation stops, RAG retrieval audit, CIM Alerts siblings, ESCU notable; inventory join; 10m user repeat; daily p95 context; OWASP LLM01-aligned severity tiers.")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=ai_ops (sourcetype=llm:gateway:access OR sourcetype=llm:proxy:prompt) earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval lane="gateway_classifier"
      | eval rid=trim(toString(coalesce(request_id,correlation_id,req_token,"na")))
      | eval uid=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(user_id,subject_id,principal_id,"unknown_user"))))
      | eval aid=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(app_id,application_id,calling_application,"na"))))
      | eval tnt=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(tenant_id,org_id,realm_id,"na"))))
      | eval classifier_score=tonumber(coalesce(adversarial_intent_score,injection_classifier_score,prompt_risk_score,"0"))
      | eval technique_id=coalesce(owasp_llm_rule_id,escu_technique,ruleset_hit,"unlabeled")
      | fields _time lane rid uid aid tnt classifier_score technique_id ]
    [ search index=ai_ops (sourcetype=openai:api OR sourcetype=azure:openai OR sourcetype=bedrock:invoke OR sourcetype=vertex:gemini OR sourcetype=anthropic:api) earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval lane="provider_moderation"
      | eval mod_stop=if(match(coalesce(finish_reason,stop_reason,""),"(?i)content_filter|content_policy"),1,if(match(coalesce(moderation_flagged,policy_violation,""),"(?i)true|1|flagged"),1,0))
      | where mod_stop==1
      | eval rid=trim(toString(coalesce(request_id,correlation_id,operation_Id,"na")))
      | eval uid=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(user_id,end_user_id,subject,"unknown_user"))))
      | eval aid=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(app_id,application_id,"na"))))
      | eval tnt=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(tenant_id,subscription_id,aws_account_id,"na"))))
      | eval classifier_score=tonumber(coalesce(provider_risk_score,moderation_score,"0.85"))
      | eval technique_id="provider_moderation_stop"
      | fields _time lane rid uid aid tnt classifier_score technique_id ]
    [ search index=ai_ops sourcetype=rag:retrieval:audit earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval lane="rag_audit"
      | eval indirect=if(match(coalesce(rag_ruleset_hit,injection_surface,""),"(?i)owasp_llm01|indirect|corpus_poison") OR tonumber(coalesce(retrieval_fanout_count,"0"))>12,1,0)
      | where indirect==1
      | eval rid=trim(toString(coalesce(request_id,rag_trace_id,"na")))
      | eval uid=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(user_id,requestor_id,"unknown_user"))))
      | eval aid=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(app_id,knowledge_app_id,"na"))))
      | eval tnt=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(tenant_id,"na"))))
      | eval classifier_score=tonumber(coalesce(rag_poison_score,"0.75"))
      | eval technique_id="rag_indirect_injection_hint"
      | fields _time lane rid uid aid tnt classifier_score technique_id ]
    [ | tstats summariesonly=true latest(_time) AS _time count AS alert_hits FROM datamodel=Alerts WHERE nodename=Alerts earliest=-4h@h latest=now BY Alerts.signature Alerts.dest
      | eval lane="cim_alerts"
      | eval rid=trim(toString(_time))
      | eval uid="unknown_user"
      | eval aid=lower(trim(toString(Alerts.dest)))
      | eval tnt="na"
      | eval classifier_score=0.6
      | eval technique_id=lower(trim(toString(Alerts.signature)))
      | fields _time lane rid uid aid tnt classifier_score technique_id alert_hits ]
    [ search index=notable earliest=-4h@h latest=now (rule_name="*LLM*Prompt*Injection*" OR search_name="*llm*prompt*injection*")
      | eval lane="escu_notable"
      | eval rid=trim(toString(coalesce(event_id,rule_id,search_name,"na")))
      | eval uid=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(user,dvc_risk_object,"unknown_user"))))
      | eval aid=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(dest,app,risk_object,"na"))))
      | eval tnt="na"
      | eval classifier_score=0.9
      | eval technique_id="escu_llm_prompt_injection"
      | fields _time lane rid uid aid tnt classifier_score technique_id ]
| eval request_id=coalesce(rid,"na")
| eval user_id=coalesce(uid,"unknown_user")
| eval app_id=coalesce(aid,"na")
| eval tenant_event=coalesce(tnt,"na")
| eval classifier_score=coalesce(classifier_score,0)
| join type=left max=0 app_id
    [| inputlookup llm_app_inventory.csv
     | eval app_id=lower(trim(toString(app_id)))
     | rename tenant AS registry_tenant
     | fields app_id owner registry_tenant classification is_external_facing red_team_window allowed_rag_sources ]
| eval owner=coalesce(owner,"unassigned_owner")
| eval tenant=coalesce(registry_tenant,tenant_event,"na")
| eval classification=coalesce(classification,"unclassified")
| eval is_external_facing=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(is_external_facing,"unknown"))))
| eval red_team_window=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(red_team_window,"false"))))
| eval allowed_rag_sources=coalesce(allowed_rag_sources,"unset")
| sort 0 user_id + _time
| streamstats time_window=600 count AS repeat_10m BY user_id
| bin _time span=1d AS day_key
| eventstats perc95(classifier_score) AS p95_cls_global_day BY day_key
| eval sys_extract=if(match(lower(toString(technique_id)),"(?i)system_prompt|sys_extract|prompt_extract|spill"),1,0)
| eval ext=if(match(is_external_facing,"(?i)true|1|yes"),1,0)
| eval rt=if(match(red_team_window,"(?i)true|1|yes"),1,0)
| eval severity=case(
    rt==1,"LOW",
    ext==1 AND repeat_10m>=2 AND classifier_score>=0.95,"CRITICAL",
    sys_extract==1,"HIGH",
    classifier_score>=0.7,"MEDIUM",
    true(),"INFO")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    severity=="CRITICAL","route_ai_risk_officer_soc_temp_ban_repeat_external_high_score",
    severity=="HIGH","isolate_session_rotate_credentials_review_system_prompt_exfil_patterns",
    severity=="MEDIUM","tune_classifier_review_rag_allowlist_and_gateway_regex_locales",
    severity=="LOW","annotate_red_team_drill_refresh_inventory_timestamps",
    true(),"continue_monitoring_refresh_escu_and_provider_lanes")
| table _time lane app_id user_id request_id tenant owner classification is_external_facing red_team_window severity classifier_score repeat_10m p95_cls_global_day recommended_action technique_id allowed_rag_sources
cimSpl:
| tstats summariesonly=true latest(Alerts.severity) AS alert_sev latest(Alerts.signature) AS sig count FROM datamodel=Alerts WHERE nodename=Alerts earliest=-4h@h latest=now BY Alerts.dest
| rename Alerts.dest AS app_id
| eval app_id=lower(trim(toString(app_id)))
| join type=left max=0 app_id [| inputlookup llm_app_inventory.csv | eval app_id=lower(trim(toString(app_id))) | fields app_id owner classification is_external_facing red_team_window]
| table app_id owner classification is_external_facing red_team_window alert_sev sig count
dataModelAcceleration: Accelerate Splunk CIM Alerts summaries for thirty days minimum so the tstats multisearch arm returns quickly when correlated with LLM application identifiers; refresh llm_app_inventory.csv whenever OAuth clients or RAG corpora change.

references.title: MITRE ATLAS — AI threat knowledge base
Splunk Lantern — Artificial intelligence and security
Splunk Docs — Alerts in the Common Information Model

knownFalsePositives: Authorized red-team campaigns and purple-team exercises produce high classifier scores that must be tagged through red_team_window in llm_app_inventory.csv to avoid overnight pages. Security awareness training corpora and internal evaluation suites that replay HarmBench, JailbreakBench, or AdvBench style strings will trip regex or score thresholds even when runs are benign. Linguistics or policy teams discussing ignore previous instructions as a citation example can resemble attacks until classifiers incorporate document context signals. Code-review copilots that paste enterprise security policy excerpts into prompts may match sensitive-pattern rules without malicious intent. Multilingual prompts that paraphrase English jailbreaks using transliterated characters can false-negative on English-only regex while still being benign homework or translation tasks. Tool-call traces may carry injection-like arguments that tools reject harmlessly, producing scary telemetry without impact. Academic datasets that include adversarial strings for benchmarking should run only on isolated sourcetypes excluded from production paging. Newly deployed retrieval indexes can spike retrieval_fanout_count during backfills, mimicking indirect injection until baselines stabilize. Provider-side content_filter stops sometimes reflect vendor threshold drift rather than user malice, so treat provider arms as corroboration not sole proof. Service accounts sharing one principal identifier can make repeat_10m look like a brute-force human when automation batches retries. Clock skew across Kubernetes nodes can distort streamstats windows until NTP is repaired.
hardware: Applies to SaaS LLM deployments on Azure OpenAI Service, Amazon Bedrock, Google Vertex AI, OpenAI organization projects, and Anthropic enterprise workspaces where gateways can emit redaction-safe telemetry before provider APIs execute.
exclusions: Does not replace UC-13.4.11 provider content-filter outcome analytics, UC-13.4.7 MCP tool-invocation governance, UC-13.4.6 self-hosted Ollama misuse detection, UC-13.4.10 API key rotation attestations, UC-13.4.5 gateway rate-limit fairness, or deep model training pipeline security. Does not itself remediate compromised models or poisoned corpora without companion change controls.

controlTest.positiveScenario: In a staging tenant, send repeated high-score injection probes through an external-facing app_id without red_team_window while gateway classifiers flag adversarial_intent_score above 0.95; confirm security_uc_13_4_9_llm_prompt_injection_intent returns CRITICAL within two search executions and that provider moderation or RAG audit arms corroborate when enabled.
controlTest.negativeScenario: During an approved drill with red_team_window true on the same app_id, confirm severities cap at LOW and paging rules suppress while audit events still land for governance.

evidence: Saved search security_uc_13_4_9_llm_prompt_injection_intent with ten-minute schedule; CSV exports of the closing table to a restricted ai_governance_evidence index; Enterprise Security episode links when notables fire.
visualization: Severity-colored table by app_id and user_id with drilldowns to gateway lane, provider moderation metadata, RAG doc identifiers, CIM alert signatures, and ESCU rule titles without raw prompt text.
